response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional geospatial expertise through GISCollective and Green Map work, plus strong full-stack learning ability, there is a critical gap in the primary requirement: modern C++ development. Their C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014-2015 and thesis work) is over a decade old and minimal in scope. Since 2015, work has been primarily TypeScript, Node.js, and D—languages far removed from systems-level C++ needed for a high-performance routing pipeline. This is a senior IC role requiring immediate C++ competency, not an apprenticeship opportunity.
strengths: Deep geospatial data expertise through founding and maintaining GISCollective platform, directly relevant to routing tile work
Proven ability to learn new technologies and work across the full technical stack, demonstrating adaptability to new domains
Passionate advocate for testing and quality assurance, with TDD-focused mindset and multiple testing infrastructure projects
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience; last real C++ work was 2014–2015 at National Instruments, over a decade ago
Current professional focus is entirely TypeScript/Node.js at recent roles; no evidence of systems-level programming or low-latency performance work
No Rust experience whatsoever, despite Rust being listed as an acceptable alternative for the primary technical requirement
